title: Brand Discovery
description: Brand identity and discovery file. Hosted at /.well-known/brand.json on house domains. Contains the full brand portfolio with identity, creative assets, and digital properties. Brands are identified by house + brand_id (like properties are identified by publisher + property_id). Supports variants: house portfolio (full brand data), brand agent (agent provides brand info via MCP), house redirect (pointer to house domain), or authoritative location redirect.

brand_id.description: Brand identifier within the house portfolio. Lowercase alphanumeric with underscores. House chooses this ID.

localized_name.description: A localized name with BCP 47 locale code key (e.g., 'en_US', 'fr_CA', 'zh_CN') and name value. Bare language codes ('en') are accepted as wildcards for backwards compatibility.

keller_type.description: Brand architecture type from Keller's theory. master: primary brand of house. sub_brand: carries parent name (Nike SB). endorsed: independent identity backed by parent (Air Jordan 'by Nike'). independent: operates separately (Converse under Nike, Inc.)

logo.description: Brand logo asset with structured fields for orientation, background compatibility, and variant type

orientation.description: Logo aspect ratio orientation. square: ~1:1, horizontal: wide, vertical: tall, stacked: vertically arranged elements

background.description: Background compatibility. dark-bg: use on dark backgrounds, light-bg: use on light backgrounds, transparent-bg: has transparent background

variant.description: Logo variant type. primary: main logo, secondary: alternative, icon: symbol only, wordmark: text only, full-lockup: complete logo

tags.description: Additional semantic tags for custom categorization beyond the standard orientation, background, and variant fields

usage.description: Human-readable description of when to use this logo variant (e.g., 'Primary logo for use on light backgrounds')

colors.description: Brand color palette. Each role accepts a single hex color or an array of hex colors for brands with multiple values per role. Beyond the core five roles, brands can provide additional color roles for finer granularity — heading, body, label, border, divider, surface_1, surface_2, etc.

font_file.description: A font file with weight, style, and variable font metadata

url.description: HTTPS URL to the font file (WOFF2, TTF, or OTF)

weight.description: CSS numeric font-weight for static fonts (100-900)

weight_range.description: Variable font weight axis range as [min, max] (e.g., [100, 900]). Use instead of weight for variable fonts.

font_role.description: A font role entry. Either a CSS font-family string (simple) or a structured object with family name and font files (rich).

oneOf.description: Structured font with family name, downloadable font files, and typographic metadata

files.description: Font files for different weights and styles

opentype_features.description: OpenType feature tags to enable (e.g., ['ss01', 'tnum', 'cv01']). These are four-character tags per the OpenType spec.

fallbacks.description: Ordered fallback font-family names for when the primary font is unavailable or does not support the required script (e.g., ['Noto Sans Arabic', 'Noto Sans SC', 'sans-serif'])

fonts.description: Brand typography. Each key is a role name (e.g., 'primary', 'secondary') referenced by type_scale entries. Values are either a CSS font-family string or a structured object with font files for reliable resolution.

property.description: A digital property owned by a brand

identifier.description: Property identifier - domain for websites, bundle ID for apps

primary.description: Whether this is the primary property for the brand

categories.description: Product categories available in the catalog

last_updated.description: When the product catalog was last updated

update_frequency.description: How frequently the product catalog is updated

endpoint.description: Base URL for checkout session API

supported_payment_providers.description: Payment providers supported by this checkout endpoint

brand.description: A brand within a house portfolio. Combines identity (who) with creative assets (how to represent). Referenced as domain + brand_id.

id.description: Brand identifier within the house. House chooses this ID.

url.description: Primary brand URL for context and asset discovery

names.description: Localized brand names. Multiple entries per language allowed for aliases.

parent_brand.description: Parent brand ID for sub-brands and endorsed brands

industries.description: Brand industries (e.g., ['automotive'] or ['pharmaceutical', 'cpg'] for a consumer health company). Describes what the company does — not what regulatory regimes apply (use policy_categories for that).

tone.description: Brand voice and messaging tone guidelines

oneOf.description: Simple tone descriptors for backwards compatibility

voice.description: High-level voice descriptor (e.g., 'warm and inviting', 'professional and trustworthy')

attributes.description: Personality traits that characterize the brand voice

dos.description: Guidance for copy generation - what TO do

donts.description: Guardrails to avoid brand violations - what NOT to do

oneOf.description: Plain tagline string for backwards compatibility
Localized taglines with BCP 47 locale codes

tagline.description: Brand tagline or slogan. Accepts a plain string or a localized array matching the names pattern.

properties.description: Digital properties owned by this brand

privacy_policy_url.description: URL to the brand's privacy policy

voice_synthesis.description: TTS voice synthesis configuration for AI-generated audio

visual_guidelines.description: Structured visual rules for generative creative systems

agents.description: Agents authorized to act on behalf of this brand. Overrides house-level agents of the same type.

brand_agent.description: Deprecated: use agents array with type 'brand' instead. Brand agent that provides dynamic brand data via MCP.

rights_agent.description: Deprecated: use agents array with type 'rights' instead. Rights licensing agent for this brand.

collections.description: Collections this person or brand is associated with. Enables bidirectional linking: a collection's talent references brand.json via brand_url, and brand.json links back to collections.

collection_id.description: Collection identifier as used in the seller's get_products responses

role.description: This person's role on the collection

seller_agent_url.description: URL of the sales agent that sells inventory for this collection. Buyer agents can query this agent for collection products.

house.description: Corporate or organizational entity that owns brands

domain.description: The house's domain where brand.json is hosted

name.description: Primary display name of the house

names.description: Localized house names including legal name, stock symbol, etc.

architecture.description: Brand architecture model: branded_house (Google), house_of_brands (P&G), hybrid (Nike)

agents.description: House-level agents that apply to all brands unless overridden at the brand level

brand_agent.description: Reference to a brand agent that provides brand data via MCP

id.description: Agent identifier (useful for logging, multi-tenant DAMs)

rights_agent.description: Rights licensing agent for this brand. Provides discovery, pricing, and acquisition of licensable rights via MCP. Use get_rights and acquire_rights tasks to interact.

available_uses.description: Rights uses available for licensing through this agent

countries.description: Countries where rights are available (ISO 3166-1 alpha-2)

brand_agent_entry.description: An agent declared by a brand or house. Each entry identifies one agent endpoint and its functional role in the advertising ecosystem.

id.description: Agent identifier (useful for logging, multi-tenant platforms)

description.description: Human-readable description of this agent's capabilities or scope

available_uses.description: For rights agents: rights uses available for licensing

right_types.description: For rights agents: types of rights available

countries.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country codes where this agent operates. Omit for global scope.

agents.description: Agents declared by this brand or house. One agent per type.

authorized_operator.description: An entity authorized to represent brands from this house. Verified by resolving the operator's domain.

brands.description: Brand IDs this operator is authorized for. Use ['*'] for all brands in the portfolio.

countries.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country codes where this authorization applies. Omit for global authorization.

photography_style.description: Photography style rules for generative creative systems. Defines how brand photography should look when selected or generated.

depth_of_field.description: Depth of field preference. shallow: blurred background with subject isolation, deep: everything in focus

subject_position.description: Where the subject sits in frame (e.g., 'center', 'center-left', 'rule-of-thirds')

preferred_aspect_ratios.description: Preferred aspect ratios for brand photography (e.g., '16:9', '4:5', '1:1')

graphic_style.description: Visual language for brand graphics and illustrations

corner_radius.description: Default corner radius for graphic and illustration elements (e.g., '12px', '8px', 'sharp'). For UI component radii (buttons, cards, inputs), see visual_guidelines.border_radius.

border_radius.description: Named border radius presets for UI components and layout elements. One of the most visible brand differentiators — Airbnb uses generous 20px, Stripe uses precise 4–8px, Spotify uses pill/999px.

default.description: Default border radius for UI components (e.g., '8px', '12px', '0'). For graphic/illustration elements, see graphic_style.corner_radius.

small.description: Small border radius for compact elements (e.g., '4px')

large.description: Large border radius for cards and containers (e.g., '16px', '24px')

elevation.description: Named shadow/elevation levels. Brands use elevation as identity — from Stripe's blue-tinted multi-layer shadows to Apple's single diffuse shadow. Values are CSS box-shadow syntax.

subtle.description: Subtle shadow for slight lift (e.g., '0 1px 2px rgba(0,0,0,0.05)')

spacing.description: Spacing system for consistent layout rhythm. Most design systems use an 8px base grid.

unit.description: Base grid unit this scale was designed from (e.g., '8px', '4px'). Informational — agents should use the named scale values, not compute from this.

scale.description: Named spacing scale built from the base unit

brand_shapes.description: Distinctive shapes used as part of brand visual identity

secondary_shapes.description: Secondary shapes in the brand vocabulary

iconography.description: Icon style system and usage rules

composition_rules.description: Layout composition rules including overlays, textures, and backgrounds

style.description: Texture style applied to creative assets

colorway.description: A named color pairing that defines how colors work together. Colorways ensure foreground/background combinations are always on-brand and accessible.

cta_foreground.description: CTA text/icon color, if different from foreground

cta_background.description: CTA button/container color, if different from accent

channels.description: Channels or contexts where this colorway applies (e.g., 'online', 'print', 'pos', 'social', 'outdoor'). Omit for universal colorways.

type_scale_entry.description: A single entry in the type scale

font.description: Font reference. Use a key from the fonts object (e.g., 'primary', 'secondary') to reference a defined font role, or a literal CSS font-family string as a fallback.

motion_guidelines.description: Motion and animation rules for video, animated display, and interactive formats

logo_placement.description: Logo placement and clear space rules for automated creative production

min_clear_space.description: Minimum clear space around the logo, expressed as a multiple of logo height (e.g., '0.5x', '1x') or fixed value (e.g., '16px')

min_height.description: Minimum logo height to maintain legibility (e.g., '40px', '24px')

graphic_element.description: A reusable decorative or structural visual element that is part of the brand identity (e.g., torn paper edges, watermarks, dividers, background patterns)

description.description: How the element is used in layouts

orientation.description: Preferred orientation when used in layouts

colors.description: Colors this element may appear in

asset_library.description: A managed asset library (icon set, illustration system, image collection). The URL is for human access; agent-facing DAM integration is under investigation.

name.description: Display name of the asset library

url.description: URL to the asset library (for human access)

description.description: Description of the library contents and usage

color_guide.description: Color guide for the asset library defining roles and palettes

roles.description: Named color roles used in the library (e.g., base, shadow_1, highlight_1, stroke)

colors.description: Map of role names to hex color values

palettes.description: Named color palettes mapping roles to specific colors

visual_guidelines.description: Structured visual rules for generative creative systems. Defines how brand photography, graphics, typography, and composition should be produced to maintain brand consistency at scale.

graphic_elements.description: Reusable decorative elements that are part of the brand visual identity (e.g., torn paper edges, watermarks, dividers)

colorways.description: Named color pairings for consistent foreground/background combinations

type_scale.description: Typography scale defining sizes and weights for different text roles. When sizes are in px, use base_width to indicate the reference canvas.

base_width.description: Reference canvas width these sizes were designed for (e.g., '1080px'). Generative systems should scale proportionally for other canvas sizes.

asset_libraries.description: References to managed asset libraries (icon sets, illustration systems, image collections). URLs are intended for human access; agent-facing DAM integration is under investigation.

restrictions.description: Visual prohibitions and guardrails (e.g., 'Never use black backgrounds', 'Do not crop the logo', 'No stock photography of people on phones')

oneOf.title: Authoritative Location Redirect
oneOf.description: Redirects to a hosted brand.json file at another URL

authoritative_location.description: HTTPS URL of the authoritative brand.json file

oneOf.title: House Redirect
oneOf.description: Redirects to the house domain that contains the full brand portfolio

house.description: House domain to fetch brand portfolio from

region.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code if this is a regional domain

oneOf.title: Brand Agent
oneOf.description: Brand represented by agents that provide brand info via MCP

oneOf.title: House Portfolio
oneOf.description: Full house/brand portfolio with hierarchy, creative assets, and properties

authorized_operators.description: Entities authorized to represent brands from this house. Third parties (sellers, platforms) can verify an operator's authorization by checking this list. Operators are identified by domain.

examples.note: Redirect to house domain for full brand portfolio

agents.description: Brand safety and compliance for all P&G brands

restrictions: Never place text over the product
No stock photography of people on phones

logos.usage: Full logo with wordmark for light backgrounds
